template_object.text:
Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question: What course of action should be taken to mitigate the attack pattern {ap:attack-pattern.name} ({ap.id})?

Answer: To mitigate {ap.name}, organizations should implement the following course of action: {coa:ap.mitigates<course-of-action.name}. Description: {coa.description}.

results.query:
MATCH (ap:`attack-pattern`), (ap:`attack-pattern`)<-[:mitigates]-(coa:`course-of-action`), (coa:`course-of-action`)

LIMIT 2000
RETURN DISTINCT ap.name, ap.mitre_id, coa.name, coa.description
ORDER BY rand()

MATCH (m:malware), (m:malware)-[:uses]->(t:`attack-pattern`), (t:`attack-pattern`)

LIMIT 2000
RETURN DISTINCT m.name, m.mitre_id, t.name, t.mitre_id, t.description
ORDER BY rand()

template_object.text:
Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question: Which data component is used to detect the attack pattern {ap:dc.detects.name} and what is the detection logic? 

Answer: The attack pattern {ap.name} ({ap.id}) can be detected using the data component {dc:x-mitre-data-component.name}. Its description is: {dc.description}.

results.query:
MATCH (ap:`attack-pattern`), (ap:`attack-pattern`)-[:`subtechnique-of`]->(pap:`attack-pattern`), (pap:`attack-pattern`)
WHERE elementId(ap) <> elementId(pap)
LIMIT 2000
RETURN DISTINCT ap.name, ap.mitre_id, pap.name, pap.mitre_id, ap.description
ORDER BY rand()

MATCH (ap:`attack-pattern`)

LIMIT 2000
RETURN DISTINCT ap.name, ap.x_mitre_platforms, ap.mitre_id, ap.description
ORDER BY rand()

MATCH (tl:tool), (tl:tool)-[:uses]->(ap:`attack-pattern`), (ap:`attack-pattern`)

LIMIT 2000
RETURN DISTINCT tl.name, tl.mitre_id, ap.name, ap.mitre_id, tl.description
ORDER BY rand()

template_object.text:
Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question: What techniques does the intrusion set {is:intrusion-set.name} employ during operations?

Answer: The intrusion set {is.name}, also known by aliases {is.aliases}, utilizes the technique {ap:is.uses>attack-pattern.name} ({ap.id}).

results.query:
MATCH (is:`intrusion-set`), (is:`intrusion-set`)-[:uses]->(ap:`attack-pattern`), (ap:`attack-pattern`)

LIMIT 2000
RETURN DISTINCT is.name, is.aliases, ap.name, ap.mitre_id
ORDER BY rand()

MATCH (ap:`attack-pattern`), (ap:`attack-pattern`)-[:achieves]->(tac:`x-mitre-tactic`)

LIMIT 2000
RETURN DISTINCT ap.name, ap.mitre_id, tac.name, ap.description
ORDER BY rand()

template_object.text:
Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question: Is the technique {ap:attack-pattern.name} (ID {ap.id}) a sub-technique? If so, what is its parent technique?

Answer: {ap.name} is a sub-technique of {pap:ap.subtechnique-of.name} (ID {pap.id}). {ap.name} description is {pap.description} <* {ap.x_mitre_is_subtechnique[_=true]} *>

results.query:
MATCH (ap:`attack-pattern`), (ap:`attack-pattern`)-[:`subtechnique-of`]->(pap:`attack-pattern`), (pap:`attack-pattern`)
WHERE elementId(ap) <> elementId(pap) AND (ap.x_mitre_is_subtechnique=True)
LIMIT 2000
RETURN DISTINCT ap.name, ap.mitre_id, pap.name, pap.mitre_id, pap.description, ap.x_mitre_is_subtechnique
ORDER BY rand()

LIMIT 2000
RETURN DISTINCT tl.name, tl.mitre_id, tl.description, ap.name, ap.mitre_id
ORDER BY rand()

ORDER BY rand()

Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question: Which threat actor or group is the campaign {c:campaign.name} attributed to, and what tools did they use?

Answer: The campaign {c.name} is attributed to the intrusion set {is:c.attributed-to.name} (ID {c.id}), aliases: {is.aliases}. During this campaign, they used the tool {c.uses>tool.name} and the malware {c.uses>malware.name}.

results.query:
MATCH (c:campaign), (c:campaign)-[:`attributed-to`]->(is:`intrusion-set`), (is:`intrusion-set`), (c:campaign)-[:uses]->(_tool1:tool), (c:campaign)-[:uses]->(_malware1:malware)

LIMIT 2000
RETURN DISTINCT c.name, is.name, c.mitre_id, is.aliases, _tool1.name, _malware1.name
ORDER BY rand()

ORDER BY rand()

template_object.text:
Instruction: You are a cybersecurity expert that has been trained to give precise responses to complex cybersecurity questions. You work in a SOC protecting data for enterprise customers helping to protect their digital assets.

Question: Describe how defenders can detect or disrupt the technique {ap:attack-pattern.name} ({ap.id}).

Answer: Technique {ap.name} ({ap.id}) can be detected using this method: {ap.x_mitre_detection}. It can be disrupted through mitigations, such as {coa:ap.mitigates<.name}.

results.query:
MATCH (ap:`attack-pattern`), (ap:`attack-pattern`)<-[:mitigates]-(coa:`course-of-action`)

LIMIT 2000
RETURN DISTINCT ap.name, ap.mitre_id, ap.x_mitre_detection, coa.name
ORDER BY rand()

template_object.text:
Instruction: You are a cybersecurity expert that has been trained to give precise responses to complex cybersecurity questions. You work in a SOC protecting data for enterprise customers helping to protect their digital assets.

Question: Explain how deception can be applied against the technique {ap:attack-pattern.name} ({ap.id}).

Answer: Deception can be applied against {ap.name} by deploying decoy systems or false targets that attract and expose adversary actions as defined in {ap.x_mitre_detection[_!=""]}.

results.query:
MATCH (ap:`attack-pattern`)
WHERE (ap.x_mitre_detection<>"")
LIMIT 2000
RETURN DISTINCT ap.name, ap.mitre_id, ap.x_mitre_detection
ORDER BY rand()

template_object.text:
Instruction: You are a cybersecurity expert that has been trained to give precise responses to complex cybersecurity questions. You work in a SOC protecting data for enterprise customers helping to protect their digital assets.

Question: Which ATT&CK tactic does the technique {ap:attack-pattern.name} ({ap.id}) belong to, and how does it aid active defense?

Answer: {ap.name} is part of the {ap.achieves.name} tactic. Understanding this helps defenders anticipate adversary goals and plan proactive defenses.

results.query:
MATCH (ap:`attack-pattern`), (ap:`attack-pattern`)-[:achieves]->(_x_mitre_tactic1:`x-mitre-tactic`)

LIMIT 2000
RETURN DISTINCT ap.name, ap.mitre_id, _x_mitre_tactic1.name
ORDER BY rand()

MATCH (ap:`attack-pattern`)

LIMIT 2000
RETURN DISTINCT ap.x_mitre_data_sources, ap.name, ap.mitre_id, ap.x_mitre_detection
ORDER BY rand()

MATCH (ap:`attack-pattern`)

LIMIT 2000
RETURN DISTINCT is.name, is.mitre_id, ap.name, ap.mitre_id, coa.name, coa.description

Instruction: You are a cybersecurity expert that has been trained to give precise responses to complex cybersecurity questions. You work in a SOC protecting data for enterprise customers helping to protect their digital assets.

Question: Which threat actors commonly use technique (attack-pattern) {ap:is.uses>attack-pattern.name} (ID {ap.id})?

template_object.text:
Instruction: You are a cybersecurity expert that has been trained to give precise responses to complex cybersecurity questions. You work in a SOC protecting data for enterprise customers helping to protect their digital assets.

Question: What subtechniques fall under attack pattern  {ap:sub.subtechnique-of.id}?

Answer: Subtechniques of {ap.name} (ID {ap.id}) include {sub:attack-pattern.name}.

results.query:
MATCH (sub:`attack-pattern`)-[:`subtechnique-of`]->(ap:`attack-pattern`), (ap:`attack-pattern`), (sub:`attack-pattern`)
WHERE elementId(ap) <> elementId(sub)
LIMIT 2000
RETURN DISTINCT ap.mitre_id, ap.name, sub.name
ORDER BY rand()
